rules.name: Reduce repetitive logging
rules.description: When editing a controller or model that has many repeated log_with_details calls (especially error logs), introduce a thin private helper method (_log_error, _log_info, etc.) and replace the duplicates. Keep the full logging depth (action, file, line, message). Only do this when duplication is obvious (>8–10 similar calls).

rules.name: Large file handling
rules.description: When a Perl module exceeds ~1500 lines or has many repetitive method patterns (duplicate subs, repeated logging, duplicated eval blocks, etc.), proactively suggest or apply refactoring. Options include: extracting helper methods, splitting into roles/modules, removing dead code, or consolidating duplicate logic. Always preserve behavior and logging depth.
